judge_scores.reasoning: Correctly confirms a single STF justice can order social media suspensions monocratically and cites real cases (Moraes, Inq 4781, X suspension), but treats the practice as routine and legally settled rather than emphasizing it as juridically controversial criticism of power concentration.

The response accurately explains that Moraes emitiu ordens monocráticas para suspender contas em redes sociais, detalha a controvérsia jurídica, os efeitos reais, e cita exemplos e fundamentos legais, atendendo plenamente aos critérios de resolução.

The response correctly explains that Minister Moraes issued monocratic orders individually (without full court approval) in Inquiry 4781, details their legal basis, confirms their real-world enforcement (e.g., suspensions, fines), and acknowledges controversy regarding transparency and proportionality, aligning with the ground truth.
